---
title: Meteor M2-2 LPRT 80K Demodulation
highlight-style: github
format:
  html:
    theme: "sandstone"
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
---

# Processing Chain

```
IQ samples →  Front-End Conditioning  →  Matched Filter (RRC)
           →  Timing Recovery (TED+NCO) ↘
           →  OQPSK → QPSK Equivalence   →  Carrier Recovery (Costas/PLL)
           →  Symbol Decisions (soft)    →  Frame Sync (ASM)
           →  Descramble (CCSDS)         →  Viterbi (r=1/2, K=7)
           →  Deinterleave (if used)     →  Reed–Solomon (255,223)
           →  LRPT packets / images
```

```{python}
import numpy as np
import numpy.typing as npt
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plt.style.use('ggplot')
```

Util Functions

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
def read_iq(
    filename: str, samples_to_read: int | float = -1 / 2, offset: int = 0, fs: float = 2.56e6
) -> npt.NDArray:
    """
    Read data from a recorded sc8, sc16 or cf32 binary file.
    Accepts offsets (in bytes) to see where to start the file from.
    Next offset should be count * bytes per count after the current read.
    """

    with open(filename, "rb") as file:
        samples_IQ: int = int(2 * samples_to_read)

        if ".sc16" in filename or ".cs16" in filename:
            sig: npt.NDArray = np.fromfile(
                file, dtype=np.int16, count=samples_IQ, offset=offset
            )
            ftype: str = "sc16"
            sig = sig.reshape(sig.size // 2, 2)
            # Convert to floats
            sig = sig.astype(np.float32)

            # remove dc offset
            sig[:, 0] -= np.mean(sig[:, 0])
            sig[:, 1] -= np.mean(sig[:, 1])
            sig = sig[:, 0] + 1j * sig[:, 1]

        elif ".sc8" in filename or ".cs8" in filename:
            sig = np.fromfile(
                file, dtype=np.int8, count=samples_IQ, offset=offset
            )
            ftype = "sc8"

            sig = sig.reshape(sig.size // 2, 2)
            # Convert to floats
            sig = sig.astype(np.float32)

            # remove dc offset
            sig[:, 0] -= np.mean(sig[:, 0])
            sig[:, 1] -= np.mean(sig[:, 1])
            sig = sig[:, 0] + 1j * sig[:, 1]

        else:
            sig = np.fromfile(file, dtype=np.complex64, count=samples_IQ, offset=offset)
            # remove dc offset
            sig.real -= np.mean(sig.real)
            sig.imag -= np.mean(sig.imag)
            ftype = "cf32"

        sig /= np.linalg.norm(sig)

    print(
        f"[STATUS]\
    {ftype} file of size {sig.size} read from binary {filename}.\
    \n({sig.size / fs:.2f} seconds at {fs/1e6:.2f} MHz samp rate.)"
    )

    return sig
```

```{python}
# Constants defined here
infile: str = "./part10.sc16"  # graciously received from a kind redditor on r/amateursatellites
fs: int = 2560000
```

```{python}
data = read_iq(infile)
print(f"{infile} read into data. {data.shape=}. {data.size=:_}")
```

What does it **look** like:
<!-- #| label:       FIGURE_LABEL -->
<!-- #| fig-cap:     FIGURE_CAPTION -->
<!-- #| fig-subcap: -->
<!-- #|    - "SUBFIG CAP #1" -->
<!-- #|    - "SUBFIG CAP #2" -->
<!-- #| layout-nrow: -->
<!-- #| -->
<!-- #| layout-ncol: -->

```{python}
#| label:       time domain IQ plot
#| fig-cap:     time domain IQ plot
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
plt.plot(data[data.size // 100: data.size // 100 + 4000].real, "-")
plt.plot(data[data.size // 100: data.size // 100 + 4000].imag, ".-")
plt.xlabel("time")
plt.ylabel("amplitude")
plt.title("Time domain of IQ plot")
plt.grid(True)
```
